Elon Musk tem-se revelado, nos ГғВәltimos tempos, um verdadeiro inimigo da democracia, e a verdade ГғВ© que um grande Estado democrГғВЎtico, o Brasil, foi capaz de lhe fazer frente, em nome dos valores da soberania democrГғВЎtica.

ГғВү um exemplo a que temos de estar atentos.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Isabella Tovaglieri (PfE). ГўВҖВ“ Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, con la scusa di proteggere la democrazia, di fatto Bruxelles ha introdotto in Europa il modello cinese, limitando cosГғВ¬ la libertГғВ  di espressione e di critica.

Avete presentato il regolamento sui servizi digitali come il garante dei diritti fondamentali degli utenti. Eppure, pochi giorni fa un mio video ГғВЁ stato rimosso dalla rete. Un video nel quale, con parole pulite e pacate, criticavo l'Europa per aver speso milioni di euro per impartire, con i corsi Erasmus, ai minori corsi per diventare drag queen o attivisti LGBT.

Quindi, zelanti attivisti del politicamente corretto sono cosГғВ¬ potuti intervenire per rimuovere queste veritГғВ  scomode, ledendo il mio diritto a informare, ma soprattutto il diritto dei cittadini a essere informati.

Quindi, vi riempite la bocca con nobili principi, ma la veritГғВ  ГғВЁ che volete zittire i vostri avversari politici per imporre la dittatura del pensiero unico, pur sapendo che esso stesso ГғВЁ il vero nemico della democrazia e della libertГғВ .

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Stephen Nikola Bartulica (ECR). ГўВҖВ“ PoГ…ВЎtovani predsjedavajuГ„ВҮi, kolegice i kolege, novi akt o digitalnim uslugama daje Europskoj komisiji nove ovlasti, zapravo, bez presedana.

Dakle, tu se suoГ„ВҚavamo s novim zamkama i otvara se veliki prostor za zloupotrebu moГ„ВҮi od strane cenzora koji jedva Г„ВҚekaju da kontroliraju Г…ВЎto ljudi smiju Г„ВҚitati. Dakle, ne moГ…Вҫemo ubiti demokraciju kako bi je zaГ…ВЎtitili. Neki u govor mrГ…Вҫnje ukljuГ„ВҚuju i dijelove Svetoga pisma, na primjer, pa hoГ„ВҮe li to biti danas sutra ilegalni govor mrГ…Вҫnje? Sam pojam govor mrГ…Вҫnje je vrlo nejasno definiran i otvara moguГ„ВҮnosti da se tumaГ„ВҚi na razne naГ„ВҚine. Dakle, vidimo velike opasnosti da obiГ„ВҚni ljudi neГ„ВҮe moГ„ВҮi izraГ…Вҫavati Г…ВЎto misle. Moramo nauГ„ВҚiti lekcije iz iskustva totalitarnih reГ…Вҫima s kojima je Europa imala, na Г…Вҫalost posla, a oГ„ВҚito ti nagoni nisu nestali.

Dakle, odbijamo prihvatiti ovaj novi akt i upozoravamo unaprijed na velike opasnosti koje donosi.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Bart Groothuis (Renew). ГўВҖВ“ Mr President, dear Commissioner, colleagues, since 2017, the Chinese state can demand from any Chinese company to help execute Chinese offensive espionage operations abroad and its operations to influence elections abroad. One hundred and fifty million ГўВҖВ“ mainly young ГўВҖВ“ Europeans have installed the Chinese app TikTok. Its algorithms help them decide what to like, what to trust and who to vote for. TikTok engineers in China decide what the trends are in Europe, in fashion and music, but also in politics. What could possibly go wrong, dear colleagues? Well, of course, first Chinese espionage can be enabled on a device. And second, our elections can be influenced by the Chinese state.

So what to do? First, this Parliament should take its task seriously and set up a serious hearing of TikTok's top executives on whether the TikTok app has a place in our democratic Union. Secondly, when politicians like ourselves decide to use TikTok, don't just reach out to those 150 million young voters, but also use the TikTok app to stand up against the Communist Party in China, its ideology and misbehaviour, or else we are complacent to autocratic censorship ourselves.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Kamila Gasiuk-Pihowicz (PPE). ГўВҖВ“ Panie PrzewodniczГ„В…cy! Pani Komisarz! Koledzy! Dzisiaj mГ…ВӮodzi ludzie gГ…ВӮГғВіwnie czerpiГ„В… wiedzГ„Вҷ z social mediГғВіw. To jest takГ…Вјe dla nich podstawowe miejsce budowania relacji. SieГ„ВҮ przybliГ…Вјa ludzi, ale niesie ze sobГ„В… takГ…Вјe olbrzymie wyzwania. W Polsce niemal 40% nastolatkГғВіw doГ…ВӣwiadczyГ…ВӮo wyzwisk w sieci. PoГ…ВӮowa z nich doГ…ВӣwiadczyГ…ВӮa cyberprzemocy zwiГ„В…zanej z wyglГ„В…dem i niesie to ze sobГ„В… olbrzymie konsekwencje dla ich zdrowia psychicznego.

Social media nie mogГ„В… pozostawaГ„ВҮ poza kontrolГ„В…, bo w ten sposГғВіb pozwolimy na skrajne, wrГ„Вҷcz barbarzyГ…В„skie zachowania. Pozwolimy na wzrost nienawiГ…Вӣci, agresji. Po prostu pozwolimy na cierpienie mГ…ВӮodych ludzi. Bo za nienawiГ…ВӣciГ„В… niestety stoi takГ…Вјe bezkarnoГ…ВӣГ„ВҮ i z tГ„В… bezkarnoГ…ВӣciГ„В… musimy skoГ…В„czyГ„ВҮ. Cyberprzemoc powinna byГ„ВҮ karana tak samo jak sГ„В… karane inne formy przemocy. Komisja Europejska powinna egzekwowaГ„ВҮ obowiГ„В…zujГ„В…ce prawo, wykorzystywaГ„ВҮ akt o usГ…ВӮugach cyfrowych do nakГ…ВӮadania kar na platformy, ktГғВіre pozwalajГ„В… na takie praktyki. Musimy byГ„ВҮ bezwzglГ„Вҷdni, skuteczni i szybcy, bo sprawa dotyczy milionГғВіw mГ…ВӮodych ludzi.

Ja jako europosГ…ВӮanka, ale takГ…Вјe mama dwГғВіjki dzieci, dzisiaj wzywam KomisjГ„Вҷ do przedstawienia szczegГғВіГ…ВӮowych przepisГғВіw zapewniajГ„В…cych nam odpowiednie narzГ„Вҷdzia do walki z tГ„В… epidemiГ„В… ГўВҖВ“ epidemiГ„В… hejtu, agresji, po prostu nienawiГ…Вӣci. To jest nasz obowiГ„В…zek.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Katarina Barley (S&D). ГўВҖВ“ Herr PrГғВӨsident! Erst heute Morgen erreichten uns wieder einmal Nachrichten, wie Russland systematisch versucht, unseren Kontinent durch das Verbreiten von Fake News zu verunsichern. Eine Moskauer Firma namens Social Design Agency hat den Auftrag, wohl direkt aus dem Kreml, auf Telegram, Instagram, X und Facebook, auf TikTok gezielt Falschinformationen zu verbreiten, um die Zukunftsangst der Deutschen zu erhГғВ¶hen und damit auch, um rechte Parteien zu stГғВӨrken, weil diese Parteien davon leben, Angst zu verbreiten.

Soziale Medien als Katalysator fГғВјr das Verbreiten von Fake News und Hass und HetzeГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ das hat gravierende Auswirkungen auf die Gesellschaft und auf die Demokratie, und es hat eben mit Meinungsfreiheit nichts mehr zu tun; es ist gezielte Desinformation. Wir dГғВјrfen nicht sehenden Auges dabeistehen. Das Internet ist ein wichtiger Raum des Austausches, das muss es auch bleiben, und es darf nicht zu einem Sumpf von LГғВјgen und Bedrohungen werden, in dem die demokratischen Werte untergehen.

Der DSA ist ein wichtiger Schritt gewesen: Wir brauchen internationale Standards, die auf sozialen Medien ein respektvolles Miteinander zum Schutz unserer Menschenrechte sicherstellen.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Catherine Griset (PfE). ГўВҖВ“ Monsieur le PrГғВ©sident, Madame la Commissaire, chers collГғВЁgues, ces derniers mois la censure sur les rГғВ©seaux sociaux s'est intensifiГғВ©e, touchant les plateformes comme Instagram et TikTok. La gauche, ГғВ  l'origine de 90ГӮВ % de ces censures, contrГғВҙlait dГғВ©jГғВ  les mГғВ©dias conventionnels par des rГғВ©glementations, mais aussi grГғВўce au capitalisme de connivence. Si nous devons bien sГғВ»r retirer les contenus illГғВ©gaux pour protГғВ©ger les personnes, il est essentiel de protГғВ©ger la libertГғВ© d'expression.

Avant qu'ElonГӮВ Musk ne rachГғВЁte Twitter, la censure frappait systГғВ©matiquement les voix de droite. On l'a encore vu rГғВ©cemment avec la censure d'Instagram contre des comptes et des mГғВ©dias de droite. Cette uniformisation et ce ГӮВ«politiquement correctГӮВ» affaiblissent notre sociГғВ©tГғВ© et nos dГғВ©mocraties. Chers collГғВЁgues, une Europe sans libertГғВ© d'expression est une Europe de la rГғВ©gression.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Adrian-George Axinia (ECR). ГўВҖВ“ Domnule preГҲВҷedinte, cenzura ГҲВҷi controlul politic al ideilor apГ„Вғrute ГғВ®n social media, definit ca spaГҲВӣiul public de cele mai multe decizii ale CJUE ГҲВҷi ale curГҲВӣilor supreme naГҲВӣionale, sunt incompatibile cu democraГҲВӣia ГҲВҷi cu statul de drept. Combaterea dezinformГ„Вғrii nu trebuie fГ„ВғcutГ„Вғ prin cenzurГ„Вғ, ci prin comunicare eficientГ„Вғ ГҲВҷi transparenГҲВӣГ„Вғ. IntervenГҲВӣia brutalГ„Вғ ГҲВҷi nejustificatГ„Вғ a comisarului Thierry Breton ГғВ®n povestea podcastului cu Elon Musk ГҲВҷi Donald Trump trebuia sГ„Вғ ducГ„Вғ la demiterea acestuia din funcГҲВӣie.

Este pГ„Вғcat cГ„Вғ am ajuns ГғВ®n situaГҲВӣia ГғВ®n care ГғВ®ndepГ„Вғrtarea lui Breton sГ„Вғ fie rezultatul faptului cГ„Вғ nu a pupat condurul ГғВ®mpГ„ВғrГ„Вғtesc al Ursulei von der Leyen, nu tentativa de limitare a libertГ„ВғГҲВӣii de expresie. ObservГ„Вғm cГ„Вғ existГ„Вғ astfel de tendinГҲВӣe antidemocratice ГғВ®n toate ГҲВӣГ„Вғrile membre. VГ„Вғ dau exemplul RomГғВўniei, unde un general SRI a susГҲВӣinut cГ„Вғ ei se implicГ„Вғ direct ГғВ®n controlul a ceea ce apare ГғВ®n social media ГҲВҷi nu a existat nicio reacГҲВӣie la nivelul Uniunii Europene. De asemenea, reprezentanГҲВӣii PNL, care este partid de guvernГ„ВғmГғВўnt, sub pretextul cГ„Вғ protejeazГ„Вғ copiii, vor sГ„Вғ cenzureze platformele online ГҲВҷi sГ„Вғ anuleze dreptul de anonimitate pe internet.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Moritz KГғВ¶rner (Renew). ГўВҖВ“ Herr PrГғВӨsident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, Frau Kommissarin! Wir diskutieren auch heute wieder viel ГғВјber X, oder Twitter, und Elon Musk. Die Kommission schreibt sogar stГғВӨndig Briefe, die sie dann auf X postet, und streitet sich mit Elon Musk. Ich glaube, wir sollten etwas weniger Briefe schreiben mit Elon Musk und mehr darauf schauen, was tatsГғВӨchlich bei unseren Jugendlichen mit TikTok passiert.

Wir wissen, dass TikTok eine China-App ist und es Einfluss von China auf dieser App gibt. Eine Studie der Rutgers University zeigt, dass Inhalte, die der chinesischen Regierung nicht entsprechen, z.ГӮВ B. Kritik an China oder UnterstГғВјtzung fГғВјr die Ukraine, im Vergleich zu Instagram massiv reduziert werden. Das ist ein Einfluss auf unsere Debatte und gerade auf junge Menschen und deswegen auch eine Gefahr fГғВјr unsere Demokratie. Islamisten nutzen TikTok immer stГғВӨrker, um junge Menschen zu radikalisieren.

Ich fordere die Kommission deshalb auf: Gehen Sie mit aller HГғВӨrte jetzt auch vor! Nutzen Sie die MГғВ¶glichkeiten des DSA, Transparenz in die Algorithmen zu bringen und tatsГғВӨchlich auch gegenГғВјber TikTok schГғВӨrfer vorzugehenГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ die Amerikaner sind da schon ein StГғВјck weit weiter.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Zala TomaГ…ВЎiГ„ВҚ (PPE). ГўВҖВ“ Gospod predsednik! Pozdravljam zaГ…ВЎГ„ВҚito uporabnikov na socialnih omreГ…Вҫjih in brez dvoma je, da morajo platforme poskrbeti za odstranitev nezakonitih vsebin. Ravno danes sem sliГ…ВЎala zaskrbljujoГ„ВҚ podatek, da se bo v Г„ВҚasu mojega govora ГўВҖВ“ v samo 60 sekundah ГўВҖВ“ na internetu pojavilo 180 eksplicitnih vsebin, ki vsebujejo otroГ…ВЎko pornografijo. In to moramo ustaviti.

Podpiram tudi boj proti dezinformacijam. Ampak kljub temu pa tukaj moram opozoriti, da v zadnjih Г„ВҚasih vedno pogosteje v imenu odstranjevanja dezinformacij vidimo cenzuro na socialnih omreГ…Вҫjih. Ne smemo dopustiti, da se drugaГ„ВҚno mnenje enaГ„ВҚi z dezinformacijami oziroma sovraГ…Вҫnim govorom. Svoboda govora je temelj demokracije in moramo jo zaГ…ВЎГ„ВҚititi.

Del reГ…ВЎitve pa se po mojem nahaja v odstranjevanju laГ…Вҫnih profilov na socialnih omreГ…Вҫjih, saj ravno anonimnost na socialnih omreГ…Вҫjih omogoГ„ВҚa najlaГ…Вҫje Г…ВЎirjenje laГ…Вҫnih informacij in nezakonitih vsebin in odpira vrata velikemu problemu cyber bullyinga. Vsak bi moral z imenom in priimkom stati za svojimi objavami.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Pierre Jouvet (S&D). ГўВҖВ“ Monsieur le PrГғВ©sident, Madame la Commissaire, j'espГғВ©rais avoir un ГғВ©change de vues sur le rГғВҙle des plateformes, la dГғВ©sinformation et leur impact pour nos dГғВ©mocraties. Les dГғВ©fis que posent l'intelligence artificielle, la dГғВ©rГғВ©gulation des plateformes et l'opacitГғВ© des algorithmes doivent nous amener ГғВ  agir plus vite.

Mais depuis hier, je suis inquiet. Oui, je suis inquiet. OГғВ№ est donc M.ГӮВ Breton? MmeГӮВ UrsulaГӮВ vonГӮВ derГӮВ Leyen, aidГғВ©e par la faiblesse historique d'EmmanuelГӮВ Macron, a dГғВ©cidГғВ© que le prochain commissaire europГғВ©en en charge du numГғВ©rique s'appellerait ElonГӮВ Musk. Je suis d'ailleurs presque dГғВ©ГғВ§u que M.ГӮВ Musk n'assiste pas directement ГғВ  nos dГғВ©bats, parce que nous venons d'assister ГғВ  une ГғВ©niГғВЁme soumission ГғВ  des intГғВ©rГғВӘts privГғВ©s au moment oГғВ№ nous devons renforcer encore les rГғВ©glementations. MmeГӮВ vonГӮВ derГӮВ Leyen a profitГғВ© de la faiblesse politique d'EmmanuelГӮВ Macron pour imposer un commissaire servile aux grands patrons de l'industrie du numГғВ©rique. Alors, plutГғВҙt que de nous soumettre ГғВ  Google et Meta, nous devons nous battre pour que les citoyens demeurent maГғВ®tres de leur avenir.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Ana Vasconcelos (Renew). ГўВҖВ“ Mr President, Commissioner, colleagues, social media can liberate the voice of otherwise-silenced individuals, and spread old and new ideas. It also enables threats to the life and physical integrity of individuals, as well as deliberate attempts to undermine our democratic systems.

Child abuse, encouraging violence on our streets, fake political endorsements, algorithms and bots ГўВҖВ“ who are not people ГўВҖВ“ that deliberately promote agendas while suppressing contrasting views are a few examples that we cannot ignore.

The Digital Services Act took an important step in demanding transparency and accountability mechanisms of very large online platforms. Individuals must be empowered with tools for assessing credible information.

Now, fighting disinformation does not excuse restricting free speech in the way we just saw in Brazil ГўВҖВ“ a full ban on a platform for refusing to take down a few accounts is a disproportionate illiberal measure only seen, until now, in autocracies. Democracies face threats by standing up for what sets them apart, not by imitating autocracies.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Maria Grapini (S&D). ГўВҖВ“ Domnule preГҲВҷedinte, doamnГ„Вғ comisar, stimaГҲВӣi colegi, este trist cГ„Вғ asistГ„Вғm la fake news ГғВ®n direct. Colegi din extrema dreaptГ„Вғ au numit acest document, la care s-a lucrat foarte mult, ca fiind cenzurГ„Вғ, deГҲВҷi doamna comisarГ„Вғ a anunГҲВӣat de la bun ГғВ®nceput cГ„Вғ nu existГ„Вғ o moderare a conГҲВӣinutului. Cunosc foarte bine, ГғВ®n calitatea mea de vicepreГҲВҷedintГ„Вғ a Comisiei pentru piaГҲВӣa internГ„Вғ, acest document ГҲВҷi vГ„Вғ spun cu toatГ„Вғ credinГҲВӣa: cred cГ„Вғ ori nu aГҲВӣi citit documentul, ori creaГҲВӣi fake news-uri aГҲВҷa cum aГҲВӣi creat ГғВ®n ГҲВӣara dumneavoastrГ„Вғ sГ„Вғ fiГҲВӣi aleГҲВҷi.

Noi vrem sГ„Вғ ГғВ®ndepГ„ВғrtГ„Вғm prin acest regulament, de fapt, activitГ„ВғГҲВӣile ilegale ГҲВҷi rГ„ВғspГғВўndirea dezinformГ„Вғrii, care perturbГ„Вғ toate alegerile, care perturbГ„Вғ, dacГ„Вғ vreГҲВӣi, scara valorilor ГғВ®n orice stat european ГҲВҷi ГғВ®n Uniunea EuropeanГ„Вғ. AГҲВҷadar, pentru mine este trist astГ„Вғzi, cГғВўnd ar trebui sГ„Вғ discutГ„Вғm altfel ГўВҖВ“ cum aplicГ„Вғm ГғВ®n fiecare stat membru ГҲВҷi cum aplicГ„Вғm uniform acest lucru, sГ„Вғ nu mai moarГ„Вғ copii. Au fost copii care s-au sinucis exact prin informaГҲВӣiile transmise fals. Au fost femei violate, au fost oameni uciГҲВҷi pentru bani. AГҲВҷadar, dragi colegi, citiГҲВӣi ГғВ®nainte de a vorbi.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Adnan Dibrani (S&D). ГўВҖВ“ Herr talman! Sociala medier ГғВӨr hГғВӨr fГғВ¶r att stanna. En tidning i Sverige gjorde tidigare en undersГғВ¶kning bland barn och unga, dГғВӨr man frГғВҘgade vad de blir exponerade fГғВ¶r nГғВӨr de ГғВӨr ute och scrollar. De svar man kunde fГғВҘ var krig, vГғВҘldtГғВӨkter, vГғВҘld, vikthets, vapen och en massa annat otГғВӨckt.

Jag ГғВӨr inte bara lagstiftare. Jag ГғВӨr ocksГғВҘ fГғВ¶rГғВӨlder till tvГғВҘ smГғВҘ flickor, nu nio och sju ГғВҘr. Jag ser hur de pГғВҘverkas av de algoritmer som finns i de hГғВӨr systemen. Vi behГғВ¶ver ett starkt EU. Vi behГғВ¶ver fГғВ¶rГғВӨldrar som tar ansvar.

Men vi behГғВ¶ver ocksГғВҘ hjГғВӨlpas ГғВҘt fГғВ¶r att fГғВҘ bukt med de hГғВӨr olika algoritmerna. DГғВӨrfГғВ¶r ГғВӨr min enkla frГғВҘga till kommissionen: Vad gГғВ¶r vi hГғВӨrnГғВӨst fГғВ¶r att sГғВӨkerstГғВӨlla att vГғВҘra barn och unga mГғВҘr bra? Och fГғВ¶r att se till sГғВҘ att inte vГғВҘr framtid fГғВ¶rstГғВ¶rs av en massa konstiga saker som fГғВ¶rsiggГғВҘr pГғВҘ sociala medier.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 

IntervenГҲВӣii la cerere

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Juan Fernando LГғВіpez Aguilar (S&D). ГўВҖВ“ SeГғВұor presidente, seГғВұora comisaria, ГӮВҝpor quГғВ© sube la extrema derecha en Estados miembros de la UniГғВіn Europea con un historial de cohesiГғВіn social? Parte de la respuesta estГғВЎ en la ascendencia de esas redes sociales cuyos algoritmos estГғВЎn diseГғВұados no solamente para difundir bulos y mentiras, sino machismo, negacionismo, toda forma de discriminaciГғВіn.

No es libertad de expresiГғВіn, es un modelo de negocio que pone las redes sociales como herramienta de comunicaciГғВіn polГғВӯtica y del lenguaje con que se hace al servicio de la ideologГғВӯa de sus propietarios. Por eso es tan relevante el Reglamento de Servicios Digitales que aprobamos en este Parlamento Europeo, pero, sobre todo, una ComisiГғВіn que garantice su cumplimiento imponiendo obligaciones y sanciones a las plataformas digitales que practiquen esa forma, no ya de desinformaciГғВіn, sino de ataque directo a los valores fundantes de la UniГғВіn Europea, a la convivencia y al pluralismo del que se compone la UniГғВіn Europea.

Por tanto, lo esencial es que los servicios digitales aseguren que las redes sociales estГғВЎn al servicio de los valores y de la legislaciГғВіn europea.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Aurelijus Veryga (ECR). ГўВҖВ“ Gerbiamas Pirmininke, iГ…ВЎ tiesГ…Ві sudГ„В—tingas debatas ir niekas nesiginГ„ВҚija turbГ…В«t Г„ВҚia, Г…ВЎioje salГ„В—je, dГ„В—l to, kad neturi bГ…В«ti socialiniuose tinkluose skatinamas smurtas arba kad visi turГ„В—tume sugalvoti, kaip riboti Rusijos propagandГ„В…. TaГ„ВҚiau dalis Г…ВЎitoje salГ„В—je sГ„В—dinГ„ВҚiГ…Ві dar prisimena, kГ„В… reiГ…ВЎkia gyventi aplinkybГ„В—mis, kuomet visa informacija yra kontroliuojama, cenzГ…В«ruojama ir yra kaГ…Вҫkas, kas nustato, kas yra gerai, kas yra negerai. Ir ar nГ„В—ra taip, kad prisidengiant gerais norais ir vaikГ…Ві apsauga yra tiesiog norima uГ…ВҫГ„ВҚiaupti kitaip mГ„В…stanГ„ВҚius? Г„ВҢia daug kolegГ…Ві kalbГ„В—jo apie tai, kad reikia apsaugoti vaikus nuo pavojingos informacijos. Bet kuomet tiems patiems vaikams yra neigiama objektyvi realybГ„В—, yra sakoma, kad Г…ВҫmonГ„В—s gali pasikeisti lytГ„ВҜ arba kai narkotikГ…Ві legalizacija yra pristatoma kaip Г…Вҫalos maГ…Вҫinimas, tai niekam neuГ…ВҫkliГ…В«va. Tai ar iГ…ВЎ tiesГ…Ві standartai visiems yra vienodi?

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Luis-VicenГҲВӣiu Lazarus (NI). ГўВҖВ“ Domnule preГҲВҷedinte, dragi colegi, din punctul nostru de vedere, lucrurile sunt foarte simple. Practic, eu constat cГ„Вғ prin acest DSA ГҲВҷi prin alte avertismente, rezoluГҲВӣii ГҲВҷi documente, Comisia EuropeanГ„Вғ ГғВ®ncearcГ„Вғ sГ„Вғ se transforme ГғВ®ntr-un fel de poliГҲВӣie a adevГ„Вғrului. SГ„Вғ nu transformГ„Вғm, dragi prieteni, aceste opinii ГғВ®n adevГ„Вғruri. Aici este o problemГ„Вғ de semanticГ„Вғ. Una este opinia, alta este adevГ„Вғrul. DacГ„Вғ vreГҲВӣi sГ„Вғ moderaГҲВӣi ceva, puteГҲВӣi pur ГҲВҷi simplu sГ„Вғ spuneГҲВӣi cГ„Вғ aceasta este o opinie ГҲВҷi acesta este un adevГ„Вғr.

Nu mai amestecaГҲВӣi conceptele pentru a vГ„Вғ putea lua dreptul de a cenzura adevГ„Вғrurile. ГҲВҳi atunci eu ГғВ®ntreb: cine sunt aceГҲВҷti fact-checkeri despre care se vorbeГҲВҷte atГғВўt de mult cГ„Вғ ei pot stabili ce este adevГ„Вғr ГҲВҷi ce nu? Sunt niГҲВҷte persoane care provin din zone poate de dreapta sau poate de centru ГҲВҷi veГҲВҷnic se spune despre dreapta cГ„Вғ este extremГ„Вғ. De ce este dreapta extremГ„Вғ, pentru cГ„Вғ reprezentanГҲВӣii ei ГғВ®ГҲВҷi iubesc ГҲВӣara? E ceva rГ„Вғu sГ„Вғ-ГҲВӣi iubeГҲВҷti ГҲВӣara? Nu este ceva de extremГ„Вғ. E ceva rГ„Вғu sГ„Вғ spui niГҲВҷte adevГ„Вғruri? Este ceva rГ„Вғu sГ„Вғ ai niГҲВҷte opinii?

(PreГҲВҷedintele a retras cuvГғВўntul vorbitorului)

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ SunГ„ВҚana Glavak (PPE). ГўВҖВ“ PoГ…ВЎtovani predsjedavajuГ„ВҮi, kolegice i kolege, moГ„ВҮ druГ…ВЎtvenih i medijskih platformi drastiГ„ВҚno je promijenila naГ„ВҚin na koji komuniciramo i sudjelujemo u demokratskom procesu.

Mi Г…Вҫelimo zaГ…ВЎtititi aktom o digitalnim uslugama nezakonite aktivnosti, a ne sprijeГ„ВҚiti ili ograniГ„ВҚiti slobodu govora. JoГ…ВЎ jednom ovo podcrtavam jer i ovdje danas bili smo suoГ„ВҚeni s brojnim dezinformacijama. Zakon o digitalnim uslugama alat je za stvaranje sigurnijeg i transparentnijeg online prostora, istodobno Г…ВЎtiteГ„ВҮi i naГ…ВЎe demokratske vrijednosti.

Jedan podatak Г„ВҮu vam ovdje iznijeti. Prema podacima Europske komisije, Г„ВҚak 83ГӮВ % graГ„В‘ana Europske unije susrelo se s dezinformacijama na internetu. Stoga je sasvim razvidno da moramo jaГ„ВҚati naГ…ВЎ digitalni suverenitet. LaГ…Вҫne vijesti, pak, imaju 70ГӮВ % posto veГ„ВҮu vjerojatnost da Г„ВҮe biti prenesene od istinite priГ„ВҚe. Zakon o digitalnim uslugama nije samo regulatorni okvir. To je naГ…ВЎ obrambeni Г…ВЎtit protiv prijetnji koje nam mogu ugroziti same temelje demokracije.

I na kraju pozivam novu Komisiju da se joГ…ВЎ snaГ…Вҫnije ukljuГ„ВҚi i u sankcioniranje onih koji ne poГ…ВЎtuju naГ…ВЎe propise.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Lukas Sieper (NI). ГўВҖВ“ MrГӮВ President, dear people of Europe, you might not know me. My name is Lukas Sieper. I'm 27 years old. Therefore, I'm one of the very, very few digital natives in this room.

I grew up in both worlds, in the digital and the analogue one. I illegally downloaded movies and music when I was a child. I was scammed by online shops. I am watching democracy and the rights of all of us being targeted by fake news and hate speech. I understand all of this, but I have no idea about the technical details.

With all due respect, dear colleagues, I think most of the people in this room have even less of an idea regarding the digital realm, regarding the technical details.

So here's my plea: listen to the people who know. Listen to the experts and don't play political games while shaping this digital world of ours.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 

(ГғВҺncheierea intervenГҲВӣiilor la cerere)

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Margrethe Vestager, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. ГўВҖВ“ Mr President, I think one of the central questions of this debate was 'What side are you on?' I'm on the side of democracy. And that was the impulse, that was the guiding star of the work done by this Parliament in approving the Digital Services Act.

Thank you for this very strong debate. Thank you for not just saying that was just another piece of legislation passed, now let the Commission do their enforcement work, because that shows how essential it is for the legitimacy of a parliament to be true online and offline, because this is what the Digital Services Act is about.

The Digital Services Act does not regulate online content. Full Stop. The Digital Services Act does not say something about what you can post. What it says is that platforms need to have systems in place to take down what is illegal, decided by the relevant parliaments in Europe. That is what it says. It as far away from censorship as it can possibly be. And that is a political game that puts our children's mental health at risk, because that prevents us from holding companies responsible for the safety of their products. Who would give their child a feeding bottle with substances in it that would change their our hormones? Nobody. So it's equally as important that the services that we use are not damaging to our mental health. And the Digital Services Act does rightfully give that responsibility to large platforms. Your products cannot be damaging, neither to mental health nor to our democracy ГўВҖВ“ the things that we hold dear.

So the Commission cannot censor. Of course we wouldn't, but also we can't, because the Digital Services Act does not regulate online content.

So it's about the system. To set it up is the obligation to have resources to have such a system. And it's about enabling people whose posts may be taken down because it is thought to be illegal, actually to protest against that, to say, I want my rights. And you can post harmful content, but you do not have a right for an algorithm to amplify that. That is not your rights. And that is fair. So the only thing that we are asking of companies where we enforce and they are numerous because they do not comply. Numerous of those companies. This is why we have the cases on X, on TikTok, on Instagram, in order to make sure that the decision of this Parliament is being respected. This is the work of the Commission. No rogue companies putting mental health, democracy or the integrity of our elections at stake. This is what this Parliament asked of the Commission. This is what we are thriving at. We are not at the end of this process, but we are getting started and it is going to work because this piece of legislation holds dear and builds on the values of this Union: the rule of law, due process.

I am really proud to have been part of this process. Proud to have been part of the enforcement of this. The successful enforcement of this important piece of legislation. I hope that you will watch over it. I hope that you will ask for sufficient resources for this to happen, because what is at stake is the legitimacy of Parliament to take decisions about what is legal and what is illegal, and protects our minors so that they can grow up to be true democrats. Thank you very much for this strong debate.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ PreГ…Вҹedinte. ГўВҖВ“ MulГҲВӣumesc, doamnГ„Вғ comisarГ„Вғ, mulГҲВӣumesc, dragi colegi, pentru cГ„Вғ v-aГҲВӣi ГғВ®ncadrat ГғВ®n timp cu respect pentru democraГҲВӣia europeanГ„Вғ. Vreau sГ„Вғ vГ„Вғ mulГҲВӣumesc, e prima oarГ„Вғ cГғВўnd prezidez acest for, ГғВ®n numele meu ГҲВҷi ГғВ®n numele romГғВўnilor. UrmeazГ„Вғ acum alocuГҲВӣiunea domnului Mario Draghi.

DeclaraГҲВӣii scrise (articolul 178)

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 		ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ JosГғВ© Cepeda (S&D), por escrito. ГўВҖВ“ En la era digital, las plataformas de redes sociales han transformado nuestras sociedades, brindando nuevas oportunidades para la participaciГғВіn ciudadana, pero tambiГғВ©n exponiendo riesgos significativos para la democracia y los derechos fundamentales. El Reglamento de Servicios Digitales (DSA) es un paso crucial hacia la regulaciГғВіn de estas plataformas, asegurando transparencia, responsabilidad y la protecciГғВіn de los usuarios. Sin embargo, debemos ser aГғВәn mГғВЎs ambiciosos.

Es fundamental reforzar las medidas contra la desinformaciГғВіn, los discursos de odio y la manipulaciГғВіn electoral. Las plataformas deben actuar de manera proactiva, garantizando que sus algoritmos no promuevan contenidos daГғВұinos que polaricen nuestras sociedades. AdemГғВЎs, debemos proteger la libertad de expresiГғВіn, evitando que la moderaciГғВіn de contenidos se utilice para silenciar voces crГғВӯticas o limitar el pluralismo.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 		ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ KlГғВЎra Dobrev (S&D), ГғВӯrГғВЎsban. ГўВҖВ“ Tisztelt KГғВ©pviselГ…В‘tГғВЎrsaim!

Az elmГғВәlt kГғВ©t ГғВ©vtizedben a digitГғВЎlis platformok ГғВ©letГғВјnk szerves rГғВ©szГғВ©vГғВ© vГғВЎltak, annak minden elГ…В‘nyГғВ©vel ГғВ©s hГғВЎtrГғВЎnyГғВЎval. A digitГғВЎlis vilГғВЎg folyamatosan fejlГ…В‘dik, olykor nem kГғВӯvГғВЎnt negatГғВӯv hatГғВЎssal a tГғВЎrsadalomra ГғВ©s a demokrГғВЎciГғВЎra, ezГғВ©rt elengedhetetlen, hogy globГғВЎlis intГғВ©zkedГғВ©seket hozzunk ezen a tГғВ©ren. A digitГғВЎlis szolgГғВЎltatГғВЎsokrГғВіl szГғВіlГғВі jogszabГғВЎly (DSA) szerepГғВ©nek megerГ…В‘sГғВӯtГғВ©se lehetГ…В‘sГғВ©get ad arra, hogy szabГғВЎlyozzuk a platformok mГ…ВұkГғВ¶dГғВ©sГғВ©t, megvГғВ©dve ezzel a felhasznГғВЎlГғВіk, kГғВ¶ztГғВјk gyermekeink jogait ГғВ©s adatbiztonsГғВЎgГғВЎt.

Ez a jogszabГғВЎly emellett a demokrГғВЎcia ГғВ©s a szabadsГғВЎg megvГғВ©dГғВ©sГғВ©rГ…В‘l is szГғВіl. A felelГ…В‘s platformhasznГғВЎlat elГ…В‘segГғВӯtГғВ©se, az ГғВЎlhГғВӯrek terjedГғВ©sГғВ©nek megakadГғВЎlyozГғВЎsa ГғВ©s a gyГ…ВұlГғВ¶letbeszГғВ©d elleni fellГғВ©pГғВ©s mind alapvetГ…В‘ feladataink. A kГғВ¶zГғВ¶ssГғВ©gi mГғВ©dia nem lehet a demokrГғВЎcia alГғВЎГғВЎsГғВЎsГғВЎnak eszkГғВ¶ze!

HatГғВ©konyan kell hasznГғВЎlnunk azokat az eszkГғВ¶zГғВ¶ket, amelyeket a DSA-val megalkottunk. A szigorГғВә vГғВ©grehajtГғВЎs ГғВ©s ellenГ…В‘rzГғВ©s kulcsfontossГғВЎgГғВә. KГғВ¶zГғВ¶sen kell fellГғВ©pnГғВјnk a digitГғВЎlis tГғВ©rben, hogy biztosГғВӯtsuk a szabad vГғВ©lemГғВ©nynyilvГғВЎnГғВӯtГғВЎst ГғВ©s a demokratikus ГғВ©rtГғВ©kek vГғВ©delmГғВ©t. CselekedjГғВјnk most, hogy a jГғВ¶vГ…В‘ generГғВЎciГғВіi is ГғВ©lvezhessГғВ©k a szabad ГғВ©s tisztessГғВ©ges online kГғВ¶zГғВ¶ssГғВ©get! KГғВ¶szГғВ¶nГғВ¶m!

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 		ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ ГҺВӣГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҺВәГҺВ¬ГҸВӮ ГҺВҰГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҸВҒГҺВ»ГҺВ¬ГҸВӮ (PPE), ГҺВіГҸВҒГҺВұГҸВҖГҸВ„ГҸВҺГҸВӮ. ГўВҖВ“ ГҺВ— ГҸВҖГҸВҒГҺВҝГҸВғГҸВ„ГҺВұГҸВғГҺВҜГҺВұ ГҸВ„ГҺВ·ГҸВӮ ГҺВҙГҺВ·ГҺВјГҺВҝГҺВәГҸВҒГҺВұГҸВ„ГҺВҜГҺВұГҸВӮ ГҺВәГҺВұГҺВ№ ГҸВ„ГҺВ·ГҸВӮ ГҺВөГҺВ»ГҺВөГҸВ…ГҺВёГҺВөГҸВҒГҺВҜГҺВұГҸВӮ ГҸВғГҸВ„ГҺВҝ ГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВұГҺВҙГҺВҜГҺВәГҸВ„ГҸВ…ГҺВҝ ГҸВҖГҸВҒГҺВҝГҸВӢГҸВҖГҺВҝГҺВёГҺВӯГҸВ„ГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҺВҪ ГҸВ„ГҺВ· ГҸВҒГҸВҚГҺВёГҺВјГҺВ№ГҸВғГҺВ· ГҸВ„ГҸВүГҺВҪ ГҸВҖГҺВ»ГҺВұГҸВ„ГҸВҶГҺВҝГҸВҒГҺВјГҸВҺГҺВҪ ГҺВәГҺВҝГҺВ№ГҺВҪГҸВүГҺВҪГҺВ№ГҺВәГҺВ®ГҸВӮ ГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВәГҸВ„ГҸВҚГҸВүГҸВғГҺВ·ГҸВӮ ГҺВәГҺВұГҺВ№ ГҺВұГҸВҖГҺВұГҺВ№ГҸВ„ГҺВҝГҸВҚГҺВҪ ГҺВ№ГҸВғГҸВҮГҸВ…ГҸВҒГҺВ® ГҺВҪГҺВҝГҺВјГҺВҝГҺВёГҺВөГҸВғГҺВҜГҺВұ ГҺВіГҺВ№ГҺВұ ГҸВ„ГҺВ№ГҸВӮ ГҸВҲГҺВ·ГҸВҶГҺВ№ГҺВұГҺВәГҺВӯГҸВӮ ГҸВ…ГҸВҖГҺВ·ГҸВҒГҺВөГҸВғГҺВҜГҺВөГҸВӮ. ГҺВ‘ГҸВҖГҺВұГҺВ№ГҸВ„ГҺВҝГҸВҚГҺВјГҺВө ГҺВұГҸВҖГҸВҢ ГҸВ„ГҺВ·ГҺВҪ ГҺВ•ГҸВ…ГҸВҒГҸВүГҸВҖГҺВұГҸВҠГҺВәГҺВ® ГҺВ•ГҸВҖГҺВ№ГҸВ„ГҸВҒГҺВҝГҸВҖГҺВ® ГҺВҪГҺВұ ГҺВ»ГҺВ¬ГҺВІГҺВөГҺВ№ ГҺВјГҺВӯГҸВ„ГҸВҒГҺВұ ГҸВҖГҺВҝГҸВ… ГҺВёГҺВұ ГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВұГҸВғГҸВҶГҺВұГҺВ»ГҺВҜГҺВ¶ГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҺВҪ ГҸВ„ГҺВ·ГҺВҪ ГҺВөГҸВҶГҺВұГҸВҒГҺВјГҺВҝГҺВіГҺВ® ГҸВ„ГҺВ·ГҸВӮ ГҺВөГҸВ…ГҸВҒГҸВүГҸВҖГҺВұГҸВҠГҺВәГҺВ®ГҸВӮ ГҺВҪГҺВҝГҺВјГҺВҝГҺВёГҺВөГҸВғГҺВҜГҺВұГҸВӮ ГҺВіГҺВ№ГҺВұ ГҸВ„ГҺВ№ГҸВӮ ГҸВҲГҺВ·ГҸВҶГҺВ№ГҺВұГҺВәГҺВӯГҸВӮ ГҸВ…ГҸВҖГҺВ·ГҸВҒГҺВөГҸВғГҺВҜГҺВөГҸВӮ (Digital Services Act - DSA), ГҺВәГҺВұГҺВёГҸВҺГҸВӮ ГҺВәГҺВұГҺВ№ ГҺВҪГҺВұ ГҺВұГҺВҫГҺВ№ГҺВҝГҺВ»ГҺВҝГҺВіГҺВ®ГҸВғГҺВөГҺВ№ ГҸВҖГҺВ№ГҺВёГҺВұГҺВҪГҺВӯГҸВӮ ГҸВҖГҺВұГҸВҒГҺВұГҺВІГҺВ№ГҺВ¬ГҸВғГҺВөГҺВ№ГҸВӮ ГҸВ„ГҺВ·ГҸВӮ.

ГҺВҹГҸВҶГҺВөГҺВҜГҺВ»ГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҺВјГҺВө ГҺВҪГҺВұ ГҺВәГҺВұГҸВ„ГҺВұГҸВҖГҺВҝГҺВ»ГҺВөГҺВјГҺВ®ГҸВғГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҺВјГҺВө ГҺВұГҸВҖГҺВҝГҸВҶГҺВұГҸВғГҺВ№ГҸВғГҸВ„ГҺВ№ГҺВәГҺВ¬ ГҸВ„ГҺВ· ГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВ¬ГҺВҙГҺВҝГҸВғГҺВ· ГҸВҖГҺВұГҸВҒГҺВ¬ГҺВҪГҺВҝГҺВјГҺВҝГҸВ… ГҸВҖГҺВөГҸВҒГҺВ№ГҺВөГҸВҮГҺВҝГҺВјГҺВӯГҺВҪГҺВҝГҸВ… ГҺВәГҺВұГҺВ№ ГҸВҖГҺВұГҸВҒГҺВұГҸВҖГҺВ»ГҺВ·ГҸВҒГҺВҝГҸВҶГҸВҢГҸВҒГҺВ·ГҸВғГҺВ·ГҸВӮ (ГӮВ«fake newsГӮВ»), ГҺВҪГҺВұ ГҺВөГҺВҪГҺВ№ГҸВғГҸВҮГҸВҚГҸВғГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҺВјГҺВө ГҸВ„ГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҸВӮ ГҺВөГҺВ»ГҺВӯГҺВіГҸВҮГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҸВӮ ГҸВ„ГҸВүГҺВҪ ГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВұГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВәГҸВ„ГҸВ…ГҺВұГҺВәГҸВҺГҺВҪ ГҺВұГҺВіГҺВҝГҸВҒГҸВҺГҺВҪ, ГҺВҪГҺВұ ГҺВұГҸВҖГҺВұГҺВ№ГҸВ„ГҺВ®ГҸВғГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҺВјГҺВө ГҸВҖГҺВөГҸВҒГҺВ№ГҸВғГҸВғГҸВҢГҸВ„ГҺВөГҸВҒГҺВ· ГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВұГҸВҶГҺВ¬ГҺВҪГҺВөГҺВ№ГҺВұ ГҸВғГҸВ„ГҺВ· ГҸВҮГҸВҒГҺВ®ГҸВғГҺВ· ГҸВ„ГҸВүГҺВҪ ГҺВұГҺВ»ГҺВіГҺВҝГҸВҒГҺВҜГҺВёГҺВјГҸВүГҺВҪ, ГҺВәГҺВұГҺВёГҸВҺГҸВӮ ГҺВәГҺВұГҺВ№ ГҸВ„ГҺВ·ГҺВҪ ГҺВұГҸВҖГҺВұГҺВіГҸВҢГҸВҒГҺВөГҸВ…ГҸВғГҺВ· ГҸВҖГҺВұГҸВҒГҺВұГҸВҖГҺВ»ГҺВұГҺВҪГҺВ·ГҸВ„ГҺВ№ГҺВәГҸВҺГҺВҪ ГҸВҖГҸВҒГҺВұГҺВәГҸВ„ГҺВ№ГҺВәГҸВҺГҺВҪ, ГҸВҢГҸВҖГҸВүГҸВӮ ГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВұГҸВҶГҺВ·ГҺВјГҺВҜГҸВғГҺВөГҸВүГҺВҪ ГҸВҖГҺВҝГҸВ… ГҺВұГҸВҖГҺВөГҸВ…ГҺВёГҸВҚГҺВҪГҺВҝГҺВҪГҸВ„ГҺВұГҺВ№ ГҸВғГҺВө ГҸВҖГҺВұГҺВ№ГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВ¬. ГҺВ— ГҸВҖГҸВҒГҺВҝГҸВғГҸВ„ГҺВұГҸВғГҺВҜГҺВұ ГҸВ„ГҸВүГҺВҪ ГҺВ•ГҸВ…ГҸВҒГҸВүГҸВҖГҺВұГҺВҜГҸВүГҺВҪ ГҸВҖГҺВҝГҺВ»ГҺВ№ГҸВ„ГҸВҺГҺВҪ ГҺВәГҺВұГҺВ№ ГҸВ„ГҸВүГҺВҪ ГҺВёГҺВөГҺВјГҺВөГҺВ»ГҺВ№ГҸВүГҺВҙГҸВҺГҺВҪ ГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВәГҺВұГҺВ№ГҸВүГҺВјГҺВ¬ГҸВ„ГҸВүГҺВҪ ГҸВ„ГҺВҝГҸВ…ГҸВӮ ГҸВғГҸВ„ГҺВҝ ГҺВҙГҺВ№ГҺВұГҺВҙГҺВҜГҺВәГҸВ„ГҸВ…ГҺВҝ ГҺВұГҸВҖГҺВҝГҸВ„ГҺВөГҺВ»ГҺВөГҺВҜ ГҺВІГҺВұГҸВғГҺВ№ГҺВәГҺВ® ГҺВјГҺВұГҸВӮ ГҸВҖГҸВҒГҺВҝГҸВ„ГҺВөГҸВҒГҺВұГҺВ№ГҸВҢГҸВ„ГҺВ·ГҸВ„ГҺВұ.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 

(ГҲВҳedinГҲВӣa a fost suspendatГ„Вғ cГғВўteva momente)

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 

IN THE CHAIR: ROBERTA METSOLA
President

ГӮВ 

4. Resumption of the sitting
Video of the speeches
ГӮВ ГӮВ 

(The sitting resumed at 13:44)

ГӮВ 

5. Welcome
Video of the speeches
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ President. ГўВҖВ“ Dear colleagues, before we start the address with Mario Draghi, allow me a moment to welcome some special guests with us: women who have stood up to the climate of fear in Afghanistan, who confront tyranny. Afghan women, who we are honoured to welcome today. You can see them in the gallery. Welcome!

These women with us are former members of parliament and senators. They are diplomats, doctors, athletes and activists. Some have been jailed; others have been tortured. Their backgrounds and experience are diverse, but their commitment to fighting for equality unites them.

And these voices, like yours, will not be silent. Your struggle will not be ignored. And as you have just seen from the reaction of the Members of Parliament, this House is your friend and your ally, and we will continue to stand with you in solidarity.

(Sustained applause)

ГӮВ 

6. Address by Mario Draghi - Presentation of the report on the Future of European competitiveness
Video of the speeches
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ President. ГўВҖВ“ Now, dear colleagues, with us today we have former Prime Minister of Italy and President of the European Central Bank, Mario Draghi, in order to present his report to us on the future of European competitiveness.

Mr Draghi, caro Mario, welcome to the European Parliament. This is not the first time that you come here, but this is the first time in your current capacity, so we are very happy and welcome you here.

As we embark on a new term, your report comes at a crucial time in our discussions. We need to turn the electoral messages our voters sent to us into a political programme ГўВҖВ“ one that is serious, one that is deliverable and implementable.

For it to be serious, it must help our industries in their digital and green transformation, incorporating every sector to enable faster economic growth, quality jobs and a strong industrial base capable of competing globally.

For it to be deliverable, it must create the right conditions and frameworks for our start-ups and SMEs to grow and stay in Europe. This means increasing productivity, innovation and speeding up investments in own industrial capabilities. It means reducing excessive bureaucracy, which risks holding us back. It also means we need to deepen our single market and ensure a level playing field through an integrated policy approach.

And for our programme to be implementable, we all need to work together on how to finance these needs. Above all, we need to invest in our citizens. In order to face the challenges of the digital age and fully benefit from the green transition, we need to equip ourselves with the necessary skills. By focusing on education, we will place citizens at the centre of our policies. When it comes to making progress on all the policies, no one should be left behind. We cannot allow ourselves, our citizens, to lose out.

So MrГӮВ Draghi, the European Parliament is eager to listen to your proposals on how we can bring Europe a little bit closer to how our people want it to be, to make their lives easier, our shared space safer, and our Europe better, stronger and fairer for all Europeans.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Mario Draghi, Special Adviser to the President of the European Commission on the future of European competitiveness. ГўВҖВ“ Madam President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, the Executive Vice-President of the European Commission, let me start by saying that I'm very honoured to be invited to speak to you in this plenary today. And I would like to thank the President of the European Parliament, Roberta Metsola, for this invitation and for her continuous support of my work. Let me also thank the representatives of the political groups whom I had the pleasure to meet some weeks ago. Our exchange was wide-ranging, frank, fruitful and, in particular, contributed decisively to shape my thinking as I was finalising the report.

Last week, I presented this report on the future of Europe's competitiveness to the President of the European Commission. The starting point is that Europe is facing a world undergoing dramatic change. World trade is slowing, geopolitics is fracturing and technological change is accelerating. It's a world where long-established business models are being challenged, and where some key economic dependencies are suddenly turning into geopolitical vulnerabilities.

Of all the major economies, Europe is the most exposed to these shifts. We are the most open: our trade-to-GDP ratio exceeds 50ГӮВ %, compared with 37ГӮВ % in China and 27ГӮВ % in the United States. We are the most dependent: we rely on a handful of suppliers for critical raw materials and import over 80ГӮВ % of our digital technology. We have the highest energy prices: EU companies face electricity prices that are 2 to 3 times higher than those in the United States and China. We are severely lagging behind in new technologies: only four of the world's top 50 tech companies are European. And we are the least ready to defend ourselves: only 10 Member States spend more than or equal to 2ГӮВ % of GDP on defence, in line with NATO commitments.

In this setting, we are all anxious about the future of Europe. My concern is not that we will suddenly find ourselves poor and subservient to others ГўВҖВ“ we still have many strengths in Europe. It is that over time, we will inexorably become less prosperous, less equal, less secure and, as a result, less free to choose our destiny. The European Union exists to ensure that Europe's fundamental values are always upheld: democracy, freedom, peace, equity and prosperity in a sustainable environment. If Europe cannot any longer deliver these values for its people, it will have lost its reason for being.

So this report is not only about competitiveness; actually, it is about our future and the common commitment that we need to reclaim it. The challenges Europe faces are complex, and as such they present us with difficult choices. But they are choices we must confront. The purpose of this report is to lay out a strategy for Europe to change course, pinpointing the priorities we should focus on, explaining the trade-offs we face, and offering pragmatic solutions to resolve them.

The report identifies three main areas for action. The first aims to close the innovation gap with the United States and China. EU companies spent around USDГӮВ 270 billion less on R&D than their US counterparts in 2021, largely because we have a static industrial structure dominated by the same companies and technologies as decades ago. The top three investors in R&D in Europe have been dominated by automotive companies for the last 20 years. It was the same thing in the United States in the early 2000s, with autos and pharma leading. But now the top three are all tech companies.

The core problem in Europe is that new companies with new technologies are not rising in our economy. In fact, no EU company with a market capitalisation over EURГӮВ 100 billion has been set up from scratch in the last 50 years. All six US companies with valuations above EURГӮВ 1 trillion were created in that period of time. This lack of dynamism doesn't reflect a lack of ideas or lack of ambition. Europe is full of talented researchers and entrepreneurs. It is because innovation often lacks synergies, and because we are failing to translate ideas into commercial success. Innovative companies that want to scale up in Europe are hindered at every stage by the lack of a single market and an integrated capital market, stopping the cycle of innovation in its tracks. As a result, many European entrepreneurs prefer to seek financing from United States venture capitalists and to scale up in the US market. Between 2008 and 2021, close to 30% of the unicorns founded in Europe ГўВҖВ“ that is to say, start-ups that went on to be valued over USDГӮВ 1ГӮВ billion ГўВҖВ“ relocated their headquarters abroad. And these figures do not include the many young, talented Europeans who go to study in the United States and found their companies there. It's a huge loss for our economy in terms of jobs and brain drain.

The innovation gap is at the root of Europe's slowing productivity growth relative to the US. So we must bring innovation back to Europe. And the report proposes to do so through reforming the whole innovation ecosystem. It starts with establishing our universities and research institutions at the frontier of academic excellence and making it easier for researchers to commercialise their ideas. (Only about one third of the patented inventions registered by European universities are commercially exploited.)

The next step is encouraging innovation start-ups to scale up in Europe by removing regulatory hurdles. This is not about deregulation. It's about ensuring the right balance between caution and innovation, and ensuring that regulation is consistently applied within Europe. A key initiative we propose is the creation of a new EU-wide legal status: the Innovative European Company. This status would immediately provide companies with a single digital identity, valid throughout the EU, and it is foreseen that these companies could then have access to harmonised legislation.

We also call for a profound review of how we spend public money on innovation in Europe. If spent wisely, public funds can be a powerful tool to launch breakthrough technologies. These technologies are often too risky or require too much financing for the private sector to undertake alone, especially in an environment like ours, where scaling up is typically difficult. Yet, even though the public sector in the EU spends about as much on innovation as the United States as a share of GDP, just one tenth of this spending takes place at the EU level. The report calls for EU spending on innovation to be expanded and refocused on a smaller number of commonly agreed priorities, with a larger allocation for disruptive innovation. In other words: increase the intensity of financing. The success of these measures will, in turn, depend on integrating the single market and Europe's capital markets, so that private investment can be reoriented towards high-tech sectors and the industrial structure can evolve.

Finally, a critical issue for Europe will be integrating new technologies like artificial intelligence into our industrial sector. AI is improving incredibly fast, as the latest models released in the last few days show. We need to shift our orientation from trying to restrain this technology to understanding how to benefit from it. The cost of training frontier AI models is still high, and this is a barrier for companies in Europe that don't have the backing of US big tech firms. But on the other hand, the EU has a unique opportunity to lower the cost of AI deployment by making available its unique network of high-performance computers. The report recommends increasing the capacity of this network and expanding access to start-ups and industry. Many industrial applications of AI do not require the latest advances in generative AI. So it's well within our reach to accelerate AI uptake with a concerted effort to support companies.

That said, the report recognises that technological progress and social inclusion do not always go together. Major transitions are disruptive. Inclusion hinges on everyone having the skills they need to benefit from digitalisation. So while we want to match the US on innovation, we must exceed the US on education and adult learning. We therefore propose a profound overhaul of Europe's approach to skills, focused on using data to understand where skill gaps lie and investing in education at every stage. For Europe to succeed, investment in technology and in people cannot substitute for each other. They must go hand in hand.

The second area for action is a joint plan for decarbonisation and competitiveness. If Europe's ambitious climate targets are matched by a coherent plan to achieve them, decarbonisation will be an opportunity for Europe. But if we fail to coordinate our policies, there is a risk that it could run contrary to competitiveness and ultimately be delayed or even rejected.

The first priority is to lower energy prices. Over time, decarbonisation will help shift power generation towards secure, low-cost, clean energy sources. But without a European plan, it will take a long time before end users will see the full benefits. In 2022, at the peak of the energy crisis, natural gas was the price-setter 63ГӮВ % of the time, despite making up only a 20ГӮВ % share of the EU's electricity mix. So even if our renewable targets are met, what this number says is that fossil fuels will still set the energy prices for much of the time for at least the remainder of this decade. We must transfer the benefits of decarbonisation faster to Europeans by making energy prices lower and less volatile in Europe. And the report puts forward, as I said, several initiatives to achieve this goal.

In parallel, we call for pressing ahead with clean energy installation in a technologically neutral way. This approach should include renewables, nuclear, hydrogen, bioenergy, and carbon capture, utilisation and storage. Increasing the pace of permitting and raising investment in grids will be the key to unlock this potential. Otherwise, by 2040, we could lose up to ten times more renewable energy generation then we lose today owing to grid constraints. From a European perspective, rapidly increasing the deployment of interconnectors should be the focus.

Decarbonisation is also an opportunity for EU industry. The EU is a world leader in clean technologies like wind turbines, electrolysers and low carbon fuels. We are also strong in green innovation. More than one fifth of clean and sustainable technologies worldwide are developed here. Yet it is not guaranteed we will seize this opportunity. Chinese competition is becoming acute, driven by a powerful combination of subsidies, innovation and scale. By 2030 at the latest, China's annual manufacturing capacity for solar photovoltaic is expected to be double the level of global demand, and for battery cells, it is expected to at least cover the level of global demand.

Europe faces a trade-off: increasing reliance on China may offer the cheapest route to meet our climate targets. But China's state-sponsored competition represents a threat to otherwise productive industries and to the promise that the green transition will bring 'good green jobs'. We will not be able to manage this challenge with black-and-white solutions.

That is why the report proposes a differentiated approach according to sectors and technologies. There are some technologies like solar panels, where foreign producers are too far ahead and attempting to capture production in Europe will only set back decarbonisation. Even if those countries are using subsidies, we should let foreign taxpayers finance cheaper installation of clean energy in Europe. There are other sectors, on the other hand, where we are open to using foreign technology and to increasing inward investment. There are still other sectors like batteries, where we don't want to be fully dependent on foreign technology for strategic reasons. And so we need to keep the know-how in Europe. That's key. Determining strategic value should take place according to rigorous criteria which avoid protecting vested interests.

Finally, there are the so-called infant industries, where Europe has an innovative edge that we need to nurture until companies are ready to compete internationally. To be clear, this should not be read by anyone as a call for blanket protectionism. Our priority is to do everything possible to make all partners comply with the WTO rules, including those who presently do not. Although some of the proposals in this report will require negotiations, they are generally aligned with the spirit of those rules. Insofar as we use trade measures, these should be careful, defensive and especially designed only to level the playing field. We should clearly distinguish between innovation abroad, which is good for Europe, and state-sponsored competition, which harms our workers. The proposals should also not be seen as a programme for defending national champions or picking winners, like some of the failed industrial policies of the past. In fact, the report argues for returning to the normal State aid regime, while foreseeing State aid only for investment projects of common European interest.

The third area for action is increasing security and reducing dependencies. Peace is the first and foremost objective of Europe, at home and abroad, And we must continue in this steadfast effort. But security threats are rising, and we must prepare. For Europe to remain free, we must be more independent. We must have more secure supply chains for critical raw materials and technologies. We must increase production capacity at home in strategic sectors. And we must expand our industrial capacity in defence and space. But independence comes at a cost. Securing critical raw materials will mean diversifying away from countries that were the cheapest suppliers in the world of yesterday. Strengthening the supply chain for semiconductors will require major new investments.

The cost of developing our defence capability will be substantial. These costs will be much more manageable if we have a strategy to reduce our dependencies and increase our security together. The report recommends developing a genuine EU foreign economic policy, coordinating preferential trade agreements and direct investments with resource-rich nations, building up stockpiles in selected critical areas, and creating industrial partnerships to secure the supply chain of key technologies. It also sets out a strategy to enhance Europe's domestic presence in the most advanced chip segment. This foreign economic policy should reflect European values and reconcile our security interests with solidarity towards middle and low-income countries, helping them to develop and decarbonise as we do.

For defence, our key weakness is the excessive fragmentation of the industrial base, compounded by a lack of coordination among Member States, unnecessary duplication, lack of interoperability of equipment. In the defence sector, common planning comes before common expenditure. EU countries are collectively the second largest military spenders in the world, but we do not help our defence and space industries to build up scale. Collaborative procurement accounted for less than a fifth of spending on defence equipment procurement in 2022. Almost four fifths of total procurement spending went to non-EU suppliers. The report therefore recommends increasing substantially the aggregation of demand between groups of Member States, as well as raising the share of joint defence procurement and common R&D spending.

In the defence sector, this consolidation of spending should be matched by the selective integration and consolidation of EU industrial capacity, with the explicit aim of increasing scale, standardisation and interoperability. However, at the same time, higher scale should not lead to lower competition. Europe has many highly sophisticated SMEs in the defence sector that could make an exceptional contribution to our common defence.

A key question that has arisen in the last few days, and that arises now, is obviously how to finance the massive investments that transforming Europe's economy will entail. Europe has set itself a series of ambitious objectives that have been endorsed by all EU institutions and the Member States. We have enshrined becoming carbon neutral by 2050 in EU law. We have committed to raising public spending on innovation to 3ГӮВ % of GDP a year. Member States that are part of NATO are committed to invest at least 2ГӮВ % of GDP on defence per year. Over the past months, this House and the EU leaders have discussed and agreed on the urgent, immediate and medium-term defence needs for Europe. And they have also set targets for upgrading our digital infrastructure as part of the Digital Decade. The report contains a bottom-up analysis by Commission staff of the investment needs to carry out these objectives, and they reached the conclusion that about EURГӮВ 750 to EURГӮВ 800 billion in additional investment will be required each year. Analysis by the European Central Bank arrives at similar figures. These investments are vital to carry out the objective of the report. But let me be clear: they are not new investment needs that the report has identified. They are the needs required to deliver on the EU existing objectives. So these numbers are nothing new. Once these objectives were agreed, the numbers followed. However, it's a lot of investment and we calculate that, on this scale, the share of investment to GDP would have to rise to levels not seen in Europe since the 1960s and 70s. And the effort would be more than double that under the Marshall Plan. So we must ask the question of how will we finance it?

Historically, investment in Europe is financed about 80ГӮВ % privately and 20ГӮВ % publicly. We asked staff from the Commission and the IMF (the International Monetary Fund) to conduct simulations to see whether we could maintain that split for such a large investment push. The results show that to finance this volume of investment, we must make progress on the Capital Markets Union, so that private savings can be channelled into investment across the whole EU. But even when mobilising private finance, public support will still be required.

Two key conclusions emerge. First, if the EU carries out the strategy outlined in this report and productivity rises, capital markets will be more responsive to the flow of private savings, and it will be much easier for the public sector to finance its share. Faster productivity growth could reduce the costs for governments by one third. Second, to lift productivity, some joint investment in key projects ГўВҖВ“ such as breakthrough research, grids, defence procurement ГўВҖВ“ will be critical, and these projects could be funded through common debt.

It is natural that these large numbers create worries about rising debt levels. It is also legitimate to be concerned about common debt issuance. But it is important to remember that this debt is not for general government spending or for subsidies; it is to carry out the objectives that are critical for our future competitiveness. And we must remember that ГўВҖВ“ and I stress this ГўВҖВ“ we have all already agreed upon these objectives. So if one objects to building a true single market, objects to capital market integration or objects to debt issuance, one objects to what? To our EU objectives.

This report has come out at a difficult time for our continent. On many key questions, we are divided about what to do. There is discontent in large parts of Europe about the direction in which we are heading, and there is considerable unease about the future. My role, as set out by the European Commission, is to present you with a diagnosis of where Europe stands, and to offer you recommendations on how to move forward. But it is for you, our elected representatives, to turn this agenda into action. We will only overcome division in Europe if the will to change receives broad democratic backing. The choices we face are too important to be settled by technocratic solutions. Our elected institutions must be at the centre of the debate on Europe's future, and on the actions that will shape it.

I trust that we can find consensus, if only because the alternatives look progressively bleaker. Europe faces a choice between paralysis, exit or integration, as I observed some time ago. Exit has been tried and has not delivered what its proponents hoped for. Paralysis is becoming untenable as we slide towards greater anxiety and insecurity. So integration is our only hope left. It is important that all of us understand that the size of the challenge we face far exceeds the size of our national economies, and we are facing a world where we risk losing not just peace, but also our freedom. In this world, it will only be through unity that we will be able to retain our strength and defend our values.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ President. ГўВҖВ“ Mr Draghi, dear colleagues, the sitting will be suspended for a couple of minutes while I escort MrГӮВ Draghi, and then we start the statements on the future of European competitiveness.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 

(The sitting was briefly suspended)

ГӮВ 

7. Resumption of the sitting
Video of the speeches
ГӮВ ГӮВ 

(The sitting resumed at 14:23)

ГӮВ 

8. The future of European competitiveness (debate)
Video of the speeches
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ President. ГўВҖВ“ The next item is the debate on Parliament's statements on the future of European competitiveness (2024/2802(RSP)).

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Manfred Weber, im Namen der PPE-Fraktion. ГўВҖВ“ Frau PrГғВӨsidentin, Herr VizeprГғВӨsident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Der Name Mario Draghi steht fГғВјr groГғВҹe Erfahrung und auch fГғВјr eine lange Karriere. Er steht fГғВјr einen Mann, der zum richtigen Zeitpunkt am richtigen Ort ist und die richtigen Antworten gibtГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ das hat er als italienischer Premierminister gemacht, und das hat er auch als EZB-PrГғВӨsident gemacht. Und heute macht er es erneut: Er legt uns einen Bericht vor, der den Finger in die Wunde legt, eine klare Analyse, eine brutale Analyse: Europa hat keine Zukunft, wenn wir jetzt das Ruder nicht rumreiГғВҹenГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ wir verlieren den globalen Anschluss.

Wir als EuropГғВӨische Volkspartei haben als Partei des Wettbewerbs, des Wohlstands, des Wirtschaftswachstums auf diese Themen in den letzten Jahren hingewiesen, und deswegen freuen wir uns, dass es jetzt so hohe PrioritГғВӨt hat. Der Draghi-Bericht wird fГғВјr uns in den nГғВӨchsten fГғВјnf Jahren eine wesentliche Grundlage der Arbeit sein, unsere politischen Ziele mit umzusetzen. Und es ist vielleicht kein Zufall, dass er am gleichen Tag hier im Parlament prГғВӨsentiert wird, an dem auch die neue Kommission vorgestellt wird, dass es uns genau dieses Bauprinzip vorgibt.

Wenn wir ГғВјber die konkreten Themen reden, dann mГғВ¶chte ich die nur schlagwortartig benennen: den Binnenmarkt stГғВӨrken, die Energiepreise wurden genannt, Innovation, Bildung, der Freihandel als eines der groГғВҹen Themen, mit dem Rest der Welt Handel zu treiben, um Wohlstand aufzubauenГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ viele Kolleginnen und Kollegen werden auf das noch eingehen.

Ich mГғВ¶chte fГғВјr uns als EVP einen zweiten Punkt noch einmal speziell beleuchten, nГғВӨmlich die horizontalen Themen: die Kritik auch an der ГғВңberregulierung, die durchgescheint ist, die ГғВңberlegung, dass Start-ups bei uns zunГғВӨchst mal nicht den Kundenkontakt haben, sondern dass Start-ups zunГғВӨchst mal mit dem BГғВјrokraten Kontakt haben, weil sie Formulare ausfГғВјllen mГғВјssen. Deswegen mГғВјssen wir ГғВјberlegen, wie wir dort besser werden: Politik, die weniger Gesetze macht, weniger Details vorschreibt, aber an die Umsetzung denkt; weniger BГғВјrokratie, mehr Vertrauen und ZuhГғВ¶ren anstatt VortrГғВӨge halten. Das ist der Politikansatz, den wir die nГғВӨchsten fГғВјnf Jahre basierend auf den VorschlГғВӨgen von Mario Draghi anpacken sollten.

Ich mГғВ¶chte noch zwei Gedanken sagen. Das, was uns derzeit am meisten umtreibt, sind die sektoralen AnsГғВӨtze. In seinem Bericht hat Mario Draghi deutlich gemacht, dass in bestimmten Technologiebereichen wir als EuropГғВӨer den Anschluss verlieren, und das, was uns am meisten umtreiben muss als Mitglieder des EuropГғВӨischen Parlaments, ist die Situation der Autoindustrie. Wir haben den Weckruf aus verschiedenen Ecken unserer AutoindustrieГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ einer Kernindustrie Europas. Hunderttausende von Jobs hГғВӨngen an dieser Industrie, und deswegen muss eine erste PrioritГғВӨt der Kommission sein, einen horizontalen Ansatz zu schaffen, damit unsere Autoindustrie eine Zukunft hat, damit die Autos von morgen nicht in China, sondern in Europa produziert werden.

Und ich wГғВјrde gerne Mario Draghi noch mit auf den Weg geben, dass wir ihn weiter brauchen, denn er hat hohe AutoritГғВӨt und er kann jetzt die Aufgabe wahrnehmen, nicht nur uns die Botschaft zu ГғВјbergebenГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ und ich glaube, im EuropГғВӨischen Parlament gibt es viel Sympathie fГғВјr seine VorschlГғВӨgeГӮВ ГўВҖВ“, sondern dass er auch zum Rat geht, damit wir auch auf der Ratsseite jetzt einen Prozess des WachrГғВјttelns bekommen. Er hat es deutlich formuliert: Europa ist die Antwort. Mit diesem Optimismus werden wir die Aufgaben unserer Zeit beantworten.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Gabriele Bischoff, im Namen der S&D-Fraktion. ГўВҖВ“ Frau PrГғВӨsidentin! Was fГғВјr ein Signal! In das Parlament, die Herzkammer der europГғВӨischen Demokratie zu kommen und nicht zur Debatte zu bleiben, zu hГғВ¶ren, was die Abgeordneten zu sagen haben, finde ich sehr schade, mГғВ¶chte ich hier ausdrГғВјcken fГғВјr dieses Haus.

Nach Enrico Lettas Bericht zur Zukunft des Binnenmarktes ist dieser Bericht ja der zweite Bericht, der eine Grundlage bilden soll zur Weiterentwicklung Europas und der Wirtschaft. Herr Draghi hat in seinem Bericht ja darauf hingewiesen, dass ein Kernbereich fГғВјr WettbewerbsfГғВӨhigkeit wirklich essenziell ist: Das sind massive, massive Investitionen, und zwar ГғВ¶ffentliche wie private Investitionen. Das ist wichtigГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ Herr Weber hat das auch gesagtГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ zu einer Zeit, wo viele BeschГғВӨftigte um ihre Jobs fГғВјrchten, wo die Kaufkraft seit Langem sinkt und sich viele Arbeitnehmer Sorgen machen.

Deshalb mГғВ¶chte ich noch mal hervorheben, dass wir auch diese enormen Herausforderungen der sozialГғВ¶kologischen, aber auch der digitalen Transformation niemals wuppen werden ohne ausreichende Investitionen. Die Hausnummer, die Herr Draghi in seinem Bericht gesetzt hatГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ von 750 bis 800ГӮВ Milliarden jГғВӨhrlichГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ unterstГғВјtzen wir. Das muss der erste Schritt sein, den wir hinbekommen. Aber die dahinterstehendeГӮВ trickle-down economy, die da durchscheint, die funktioniert nicht; das hat sich gezeigt. Deshalb glauben wir, dass ein Bereich total unterbelichtet ist, dass nГғВӨmlich die Unternehmen, die starken sozialen Dialog haben, die Mitbestimmung derГӮВ BeschГғВӨftigten haben, innovativer sindГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ WettbewerbsfГғВӨhigkeitГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ und dass wir auch das stГғВӨrken mГғВјssen.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Jean-Paul Garraud, au nom du groupe PfE. ГўВҖВ“ Madame la PrГғВ©sidente, bien que missionnГғВ© par la Commission europГғВ©enne, M.ГӮВ Draghi dresse un constat accablant sur la situation de la compГғВ©titivitГғВ© en Europe: les prix de l'ГғВ©lectricitГғВ© explosent, la transition ГғВ©nergГғВ©tique, dans son ГғВ©tat actuel, est un ГғВ©chec, l'innovation technologique stagne. Mais ГғВ  quoi ont pu servir les milliards investis par l'Union europГғВ©enne en matiГғВЁre de compГғВ©titivitГғВ© jusqu'ici?

Le rapport Draghi, c'est aussi la confirmation de ce que, en France, le Rassemblement national propose depuis des annГғВ©es. Nous avons pourtant ГғВ©tГғВ© tellement critiquГғВ©s et discrГғВ©ditГғВ©s! M.ГӮВ Draghi, en effet, dГғВ©fend, comme nous, le nuclГғВ©aire comme ГғВ©lГғВ©ment clГғВ© pour assurer la transition ГғВ©nergГғВ©tique. Il dГғВ©nonce, comme nous, le nombre incalculable de normes europГғВ©ennes qui encombrent les ГғВүtats membres et asphyxient nos entreprises. Il traite, comme nous, de la nГғВ©cessitГғВ© de dГғВ©coupler les prix de l'ГғВ©lectricitГғВ© de ceux du gaz, et ainsi de suite.

Le problГғВЁme, nГғВ©anmoins, avec ce rapport, c'est que, s'il est possible de partager certains constats, les solutions prГғВ©sentГғВ©es ne sont absolument pas les bonnes. La politique europГғВ©enne en matiГғВЁre de compГғВ©titivitГғВ© est mauvaise. Il faut pourtant, pour M.ГӮВ Draghi, encore plus d'Europe. C'est donc une nouvelle fuite en avant qui est proposГғВ©e: toujours et encore plus d'endettement, avec des plans financГғВ©s ГғВ  coup de milliards que nous n'avons pas et qui seront ГғВ  la charge des gГғВ©nГғВ©rations futures, et toujours moins de souverainetГғВ© pour les ГғВүtats membres, avec par exemple la fin du droit de veto pour nos nations. Et que dire de l'insistance de ce rapport sur la nГғВ©cessitГғВ© d'une Europe de la dГғВ©fense qui sacrifiera nos souverainetГғВ©s nationales au profit d'une armГғВ©e europГғВ©enne qui ne rГғВ©pondra qu'ГғВ  des intГғВ©rГғВӘts supranationaux, quelquefois incompatibles avec les intГғВ©rГғВӘts et la protection de nos peuples?

L'Europe europГғВ©enne ne peut continuer ГғВ  nous entraГғВ®ner dans ce cycle maastrichtien oГғВ№, bien qu'elle ait dГғВ©montrГғВ© toutes ses carences et ses ГғВ©checs, elle propose d'aller encore plus loin. La seule solution est de changer de systГғВЁme, grГғВўce ГғВ  une Europe des nations, libre et souveraine, capable de s'unir et de coopГғВ©rer dans des projets d'intГғВ©rГғВӘt gГғВ©nГғВ©ral. C'est cette conclusion que M.ГӮВ Draghi aurait dГғВ» livrer aprГғВЁs les constats qu'il a rГғВ©alisГғВ©s.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Nicola Procaccini, a nome del gruppo ECR. ГўВҖВ“ Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, parlare di competitivitГғВ  oggi in Aula ГғВЁ giusto. ГғВҲ tardi.

Ringrazio ilГӮВ presidente Draghi per il suo prezioso contributo di analisi. Mi spiace solo che arrivi dopo, anzi, a causa degli errori compiuti durante una stagione politica, la scorsa, dominata dalle sinistre rosse e verdi.

Per anni abbiamo chiesto di utilizzare il buon senso nel perseguire un obiettivo giusto e condivisibile come la conservazione dell'ambiente. Per anni abbiamo lottato contro provvedimenti che apparivano furiosamente radicali e miopi, guardando al quadro geopolitico internazionale.ГӮВ Il risultato di quegli errori ГғВЁ nel rapporto Draghi: una perdita di competitivitГғВ  brusca, pesante, ma non inaspettata, a cui non corrisponde il beneficio per il pianeta che tutti noi vorremmo.

Il 2023 ГғВЁ stato l'anno con le maggiori emissioni globali di CO2 di sempre, nonostante il crollo delle emissioni dell'Unione europea. Siamo l'unica grande regione al mondo ad aver introdotto un prezzo alle proprie emissioni di CO2, che si traduce in un aumento dei costi di produzione e del prezzo finale, dentro un mercato che ГғВЁ sempre piГғВ№ globale, ma anche sempre piГғВ№ disomogeneo nelle condizioni di partenza.

ГғВҲ stato giГғВ  detto: le imprese europee devono far fronte a un costo dell'energia elettrica due/tre volte superiore a quello degli Stati Uniti, per non parlare della Cina, il prezzo del gas naturale da noi ГғВЁ cinque volte superiore a quello degli Stati Uniti. Certo, con grandi investimenti saremmo ancora in grado di cambiare la rotta, ma se l'Unione europea continua ad autosabotare la propria economia, sarГғВ  come voler svuotare il mare con un cucchiaino.

Dobbiamo tornare consapevoli che la conservazione dell'ambiente, cosГғВ¬ come le politiche di inclusione sociale sono un privilegio che possono permettersi soltanto le economie prospere e dinamiche. Anche stamattina, nella conferenza dei presidenti di gruppo, mi ГғВЁ capitato di sentire dalla sinistra parole che tradiscono un sentimento di fastidio nei confronti della competitivitГғВ  economica, come se fosse qualcosa da stigmatizzare, da contenere, da sacrificare sull'altare dell'ambientalismo, qualcosa di sinistramente simile al concetto di "decrescita felice" con cui abbiamo avuto a che fare qualche anno fa. ГғВҲ il socialismo che rientra dalla finestra dopo essere uscito dalla porta nel secolo scorso.

All'opposto, noi crediamo che serva un'autolimitazione dell'attivitГғВ  legislativa e regolatoria europea. Oggi, come ieri, noi crediamo nell'innovazione, nella neutralitГғВ  tecnologica e nel libero mercato. Noi crediamo negli esseri umani che, se vengono lasciati liberi di inseguire la propria felicitГғВ , fanno crescere il mondo.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Morten LГғВёkkegaard, for Renew-Gruppen. ГўВҖВ“ Fru formand! I mit hjemland Danmark er vi meget stolte af, at vi har Europas mest vГғВҰrdifulde virksomhed, nemlig Novo Nordisk. DesvГғВҰrre er Novo Nordisk nok undtagelsen, der bekrГғВҰfter reglen, nemlig at der ikke rigtig er noget i Danmark eller i Europa, der er stГғВёrst. Og hvis Europa har stГғВёrre virksomheder, ja, sГғВҘ er der i hvert fald fare for, at de snart planlГғВҰgger at forlade kontinentet. Konklusionerne i Mario Draghis rapport er derfor en kГғВҰrkommen anledning til at skifte spor. Fra nu af skal det handle om moderne vГғВҰkst. I Renew Europe stГғВёtter vi den antagelse, den analyse. Ikke vГғВҰkst i modsГғВҰtning til bedre klima eller ordentlige arbejdsforhold. Nej, vГғВҰkst, som gГғВҘr hГғВҘnd i hГғВҘnd med grГғВёn omstilling og udvikling til et bedre samfund. VГғВҰkst krГғВҰver jo som bekendt konkurrenceevne. Det krГғВҰver klarsyn og vilje til at forandre, nГғВҘr det gГғВҰlder konkurrenceevne. Vi skal have et enklere og mere smidigt marked. Den nye implementeringskommissГғВҰr er i denne forbindelse et godt fГғВёrste skridt, hvis jeg mГғВҘ kommentere lidt pГғВҘ den nye kommission, vi har set i dag. Vores smГғВҘ og mellemstore virksomheder skal have mindre bГғВёvl og lettere adgang til penge. Og det, kГғВҰre venner, det bliver ikke nemt. Det kommer til at koste. Det er en bunden opgave, som Mario Draghi var inde pГғВҘ. Vi har ikke rГғВҘd til at fejle. Det handler om fremtiden for vores europГғВҰiske velfГғВҰrdsstater, det handler om vores sikkerhed, og det handler om kampen mod klimaГғВҰndringerne. Jeg, og vi i Renew Europe er optimister pГғВҘ Europas vegne. Vi har vist, hvad vi kan, og hvad vi skal gГғВёre, nГғВҘr det er nГғВёdvendigt.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Bas Eickhout, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. ГўВҖВ“ MadamГӮВ President, dear colleagues, we don't have to look far for an industry in crisis. In the heart of Europe, 3ГӮВ 000 workers building state-of-the-art electric vehicles are possibly losing their jobs at Audi Brussels.

This is not an incident, but a key example of the fundamental challenge Europe's industry is facing. For decades, Europe has disregarded its industrial policy, closing its eyes for the new economic and geopolitical reality. And that's what MrГӮВ Draghi called the paralysis.

And when we opened the eyes, China and the US had built new industrial bases, exporting clean tech and digital innovations. Now that Europe has realised the future of das Auto is electric, it feels like it might be too late. But it does not have to be too late, colleagues.

MrГӮВ Draghi is offering us some examples and answers to that. Decarbonisation can and should be a source of competitiveness and the driver for new industrial development within Europe. That's green industrial policy that's at the heart of Europe and should be.

And we can offer Europeans a future and millions of green-collar jobs. And this is possible, but we need to do a couple of things, and that's also what MrГӮВ Draghi said: build a true European power grid and affordable renewable energy for both citizens and industry. We need to finally start coordinating cross-border connections and use our market power on the world stage.

Second, modernise our industrial base, providing green steel, paper, chemicals. We will need investments in all Member States and not only the big ones. And to my German Christian Democratic colleagues, an Alleingang will rip up the single market of Europe.

And secure European production of clean tech like heat pumps, vehicles and wind turbines mentioned by MrГӮВ Draghi as well. We have to create a demand for European production. Europeans have to be able to afford European solutions.

But there is also criticism to give to the report. A thriving future is not built on technology and capital alone. It's the people and our environment who are the real source of prosperity. How would we farm without nature? What would we drink without clean water? What would we produce without people, quality jobs and a more equal society?

Europe has to be more than a great place to invest in; it has to be the best place to work and to live. And that is our common assignment. We have done it before, from Marshall to Draghi, let's rebuild Europe's economy, and stop the paralysis and dare to dream of a new green economy.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Manon Aubry, au nom du groupe The Left. ГўВҖВ“ Madame la PrГғВ©sidente, chers collГғВЁgues, ГғВҙtez-moi d'un doute: quand MarioГӮВ Draghi parle de lente agonie de l'Union europГғВ©enne, il s'agit bien de l'agonie causГғВ©e par un modГғВЁle ГғВ©conomique usГғВ© jusqu'ГғВ  la corde? Et pourquoi, dans ce cas, ne pas en tirer toutes les consГғВ©quences qui s'imposent?

Parce que j'ai bien regardГғВ© les 400ГӮВ pages du rapport de M.ГӮВ Draghi ГўВҖВ“ j'aurais aimГғВ© d'ailleurs qu'il puisse siГғВ©ger ici pour avoir ce dГғВ©bat avec nous ГўВҖВ“ et il y a une chose qui m'a frappГғВ©e, c'est le niveau de contradiction. Par exemple, comment oser parler de relance de la politique industrielle sans proposer une rupture avec le libre-ГғВ©change qui installe une concurrence dГғВ©loyale et fait venir des marchandises des quatre coins du monde, que ce soit nos vГғВӘtements ou nos mГғВ©dicaments? Pire, MarioГӮВ Draghi propose mГғВӘme d'abandonner des industries entiГғВЁres, comme celle des panneaux solaires.

Comment oser aussi critiquer, ГғВ  juste titre, les prix trop ГғВ©levГғВ©s de l'ГғВ©nergie sans inciter ГғВ  sortir du marchГғВ© de l'ГғВ©nergie, du marchГғВ© de l'ГғВ©lectricitГғВ©, et reprendre la main sur tous les biens et services essentiels mis ГғВ  mal par le dogme de la concurrence? Comment oser ensuite appeler ГғВ  des investissements supplГғВ©mentaires sans remettre en question les rГғВЁgles d'austГғВ©ritГғВ© budgГғВ©taire qui empГғВӘchent justement d'investir dans nos services publics, notre protection sociale, nos infrastructures? Comment oser enfin parler d'ambition ГғВ©cologique sans ГғВ©voquer un seul instant l'impГғВ©ratif de sobriГғВ©tГғВ©, ni proposer de soumettre le secteur privГғВ© ГғВ  de strictes conditionnalitГғВ©s ГғВ©cologiques? Pire encore, MarioГӮВ Draghi appelle mГғВӘme ГғВ  dГғВ©rГғВ©guler et ГғВ  jeter au feu certaines des lГғВ©gislations environnementales.

Dans ce tableau de l'avenir de l'ГғВ©conomie europГғВ©enne, productivitГғВ©, croissance et nГғВ©olibГғВ©ralisme restent les maГғВ®tres-mots, et les multinationales, les seuls maГғВ®tres ГғВ  bord. Mais je vais vous faire une confidence. Vous avez beau faire, chers collГғВЁgues, comme je l'entends ici, de la compГғВ©titivitГғВ© l'alpha et l'omГғВ©ga de toutes les politiques europГғВ©ennes, la compГғВ©titivitГғВ©, elle, ne se mange pas, ne se boit pas, ne permet pas de se loger ni de se chauffer et pas non plus de respirer un air pur.

Alors, quitte ГғВ  ГғВ©crire 400ГӮВ pages, j'aurais prГғВ©fГғВ©rГғВ© que M.ГӮВ Draghi s'obstine ГғВ  dГғВ©crire comment notre ГғВ©conomie peut satisfaire les besoins sociaux, ГғВ©radiquer la pauvretГғВ© et les inГғВ©galitГғВ©s tout en rГғВ©pondant ГғВ  l'urgence ГғВ©cologique. Car sinon, ce n'est pas une lente agonie mais une trГғВЁs rapide chute qui nous attend.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Alexander Sell, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. ГўВҖВ“ Frau PrГғВӨsidentin! Herr Draghi stellt einen neuen Plan vor; das ist nichts Neues. Schon im Jahr 2000 hat die Kommission so einen Plan vorgelegt, die sogenannte LissabonГўВҖВ‘Agenda. Der Plan damals: Europa zurГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ Zitat ГўВҖВ“ ГўВҖВһwettbewerbsfГғВӨhigsten und dynamischsten Wirtschaft der Welt zu machen, bis spГғВӨtestens 2010ГўВҖВң. Und, was ist daraus geworden? Nichts. Vor 25ГӮВ Jahren waren die amerikanische Wirtschaft und die europГғВӨische Wirtschaft etwa gleich groГғВҹ. Heute ist das Bruttoinlandsprodukt der Amerikaner 30ГӮВ % grГғВ¶ГғВҹer als unseres.

Herr Draghi ist fГғВјr diesen Niedergang wesentlich mitverantwortlich. Inflation und Preissteigerungen sind auch eine Folge der Politik, die er als Chef der EuropГғВӨischen Zentralbank verfolgt hat. In Deutschland explodieren die Mieten, Obst und GemГғВјse wird unbezahlbar, 5ГӮВ Millionen Rentner in Deutschland haben weniger als 1ГӮВ 000 Euro im Monat. Deshalb lehnen wir die vorgeschlagene Aufnahme weiterer Schulden durch die EuropГғВӨische Union ab. Wir wollen keine europГғВӨische Schuldenunion. Deutschland ist kein reiches Land mehr, wir kГғВ¶nnen nicht lГғВӨnger fГғВјr die Schulden anderer LГғВӨnder haften.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Markus Ferber (PPE). ГўВҖВ“ Frau PrГғВӨsidentin, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen, liebe Kollegen! Mit dem DraghiГўВҖВ‘Bericht ist das Thema WettbewerbsfГғВӨhigkeit endlich wieder dort angekommen, wo es wirklich hingehГғВ¶rtГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ hier ins EuropГғВӨische Parlament und natГғВјrlich auch auf die Tagesordnung der StaatsГўВҖВ‘ und Regierungschefs. In der Tat mГғВјssen wir uns ehrgeizige Ziele setzen, wenn wir ein wettbewerbsfГғВӨhiger Wirtschaftsraum sein wollen, wenn wir einer werden wollen und wenn wir damit gerade den jungen Menschen eine Perspektive hier in Europa geben wollen.

Eine SГғВӨule der Strategie fГғВјr mehr WettbewerbsfГғВӨhigkeit ist auch das Thema Finanzierung. Jeder weiГғВҹ: Unser Finanzierungsmodell hat eine Schlagseite hin zur Bankenfinanzierung. Auf der Kapitalmarktseite geht fast gar nichts. BГғВ¶rsengГғВӨnge europГғВӨischer Wachstumsunternehmen finden nicht in Frankfurt statt, sie finden nicht in Paris statt, sie finden nicht in Mailand stattГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ sie finden in New York statt, um nur eines der Probleme zu benennen. Deswegen muss die Kapitalmarktunion ein Teil der Antwort auf unsere Herausforderungen sein.

Im Bericht von Mario Draghi ist der Ernst der Lage sehr klar beschrieben, und es sind auch klare Handlungsoptionen aufgezeigt. Ich denke, viele der LГғВ¶sungswege, die hier aufgeschrieben wurden, sind uns eigentlich schon lГғВӨngst bekannt. Das zeigt aber: Wir haben in Europa kein Erkenntnisproblem, wir haben ein Umsetzungsproblem. Es fehlt an politischem WillenГӮВ ГўВҖВ“ und der fehlt nicht hier im Hause, sondern der fehlt im Rat, der fehlt bei den StaatsГўВҖВ‘ und Regierungschefs. Das gilt angefangen beim Thema Aufsichtsrecht ГғВјber das Thema Steuern bis hin zum Thema Insolvenzrecht. Wir diskutieren das seit vielen, vielen Jahren hier; bewegt hat sich faktisch nichts.

Dass der gemeinsame Binnenmarkt der Wachstumsmotor sein muss, ist, glaube ich, jedem klar, aber in den letzten Jahren haben wir eher Desintegration im Binnenmarkt erlebt als Integration. Deswegen mein Appell an die Kommission, an die Mitgliedstaaten: Lassen Sie uns das Momentum nutzen, die VorschlГғВӨge aus dem DraghiГўВҖВ‘Bericht umsetzen, ProduktivitГғВӨtswachstum schaffen, Binnenmarkt und Kapitalmarktunion voranbringen! Dann brauchen wir auch keine neuen Schulden.

ГӮВ 
ГӮВ ГӮВ 
ГӮВ 

ГӮВ ГӮВ Mohammed Chahim (S&D). ГўВҖВ“ Voorzitter, de heer Draghi zei dat investeringen nodig zijn om onze bestaande ambities en plannen na te streven. Het gaat dus niet om nieuw geld. Dit vind ik een heel interessante boodschap.

We moeten niet denken dat we het allemaal alleen kunnen en dat de lidstaten alleen voldoende concurrentie kunnen bieden als we niet echt samenwerken. Nederlandse, Belgische en Duitse bedrijven leveren aan zeer veel bedrijven in Europa, en andere Europese bedrijven leveren weer aan bedrijven in Nederland, BelgiГғВ« en Duitsland. We vormen samen ГғВ©ГғВ©n keten, die sterker wordt als we de volledige keten versterken. Doen we dat niet, dan zijn we zo sterk als de zwakste schakel.

Om de Nederlandse, Belgische en Duitse industrie te steunen en toekomstbestendig te maken, moeten we groen industriebeleid voeren. Dit vereist investeringen en ondernemerschap van de overheid. We moeten Europeanen als overheid de kansrijke technologieГғВ«n laten zien waarin wij vertrouwen hebben.
